articles.headline: India-EU FTA Negotiations See Major Breakthrough, Deal Expected Soon
articles.explainer:
• After years of intense negotiations, India and the European Union have reportedly reached a significant breakthrough on key contentious issues concerning the proposed Free Trade Agreement (FTA).
• Sources indicate progress on intellectual property rights, data protection, and market access for certain agricultural and automotive products.
• The agreement aims to boost bilateral trade and investment, potentially creating one of the largest trade blocs globally.
• A joint statement from lead negotiators highlighted a shared commitment to a balanced and mutually beneficial outcome.
• The finalization of the FTA could mark a strategic shift in India's trade policy, diversifying its economic partnerships.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: India-EU FTA negotiations started in 2007, paused in 2013, and resumed in 2022.
Key areas of negotiation include goods, services, investment, IPR, and geographical indications.
The EU is one of India's largest trading partners.
FTA aims to reduce tariffs and non-tariff barriers.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the India-EU FTA for India's economic growth and geopolitical influence.
Analyze the potential challenges and opportunities for Indian industries post-FTA implementation, particularly in sectors like agriculture, automotive, and IT.
Evaluate how the FTA can strengthen India's position in global supply chains and diversify its trade relationships beyond traditional partners.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union symbolizing trade partnership.
articles.trivia: The EU is a customs union and single market, comprising 27 member states, making it the world's third-largest economy by nominal GDP.

articles.headline: Veteran Politician Mukul Roy Passes Away at 71
articles.explainer:
• Mukul Roy, a prominent political figure known for his significant contributions to West Bengal politics and Indian Railways, passed away at the age of 71.
• Roy served as a Member of Parliament (Rajya Sabha) from 2006 to 2017 and held the crucial portfolio of Minister of Railways in 2012.
• He was also a sitting Member of the Legislative Assembly (MLA) in West Bengal since 2021, representing the Krishnanagar Uttar constituency.
• Beginning his career with the Indian National Congress, Roy later became a founding member of the Trinamool Congress (TMC) and held several key positions within the party.
• His political journey also saw a brief stint with the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) before returning to the TMC, highlighting his dynamic political career.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
prelimsPoints: Mukul Roy served as Union Minister of Railways in 2012.
He was an MP (Rajya Sabha) from 2006-2017.
He was an MLA in West Bengal since 2021.
Known for his association with Trinamool Congress and a brief period with BJP.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of seasoned politicians like Mukul Roy in shaping regional and national political discourse.
Analyze the impact of defections and re-defections of political leaders on party stability and democratic processes in India.
Evaluate the challenges faced by regional parties in maintaining their identity amidst the dominance of national parties, using examples from West Bengal politics.
articles.imageDescription: Portrait of Mukul Roy, a veteran Indian politician.
articles.trivia: Mukul Roy was instrumental in the growth of the Trinamool Congress (TMC) and was often referred to as Mamata Banerjee's second-in-command during the party's early years.

articles.headline: Air Ambulance Crash Near Ranchi Kills All Seven On Board; DGCA Orders Probe
articles.explainer:
• A Beechcraft C90 air ambulance operated by Redbird Airways crashed into a forest shortly after takeoff from Ranchi's Birsa Munda Airport.
• The tragic incident occurred during a medical evacuation mission, resulting in the immediate death of all seven individuals on board, including patients, medical staff, and crew.
• Preliminary reports suggest engine trouble or a sudden loss of control as potential causes, though a detailed investigation is underway.
• The Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) has initiated a high-level inquiry to ascertain the exact reasons behind the crash and to review aviation safety protocols for air ambulances.
• This incident reignites concerns about the safety standards and regulatory oversight of non-scheduled air operations, particularly those involving critical medical transport.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: The crash involved a Beechcraft C90 air ambulance.
Incident occurred near Ranchi, Jharkhand.
DGCA is the primary regulatory body for civil aviation safety in India.
Air ambulance services fall under non-scheduled air operations.
mainsPoints: Examine the regulatory framework and safety challenges associated with air ambulance services in India.
Discuss measures that can be implemented to enhance aviation safety standards for non-scheduled flights, particularly in critical medical transport.
Analyze the role of technology and robust maintenance protocols in preventing such aviation accidents and ensuring passenger safety.
articles.imageDescription: Rescue teams inspecting the wreckage of an aircraft in a forested area.
articles.trivia: The Beechcraft C90 King Air is a popular turboprop aircraft often used for utility and light transport roles, including air ambulance configurations, due to its reliability and short-field performance.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds States' Rights in Landmark Fiscal Devolution Case
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a significant judgment, affirming the constitutional principle of fiscal federalism by ruling in favor of several states regarding the Centre's allocation of financial resources.
• The case centered on the interpretation of Article 280 (Finance Commission) and the extent of the Union government's discretion in distributing tax revenues and grants-in-aid.
• The Court emphasized the need for a balanced approach to federal financial relations, ensuring states have adequate resources for developmental activities without undue central interference.
• This ruling is expected to have far-reaching implications for Centre-State financial dynamics, potentially leading to a more equitable distribution of funds.
• Legal experts suggest the judgment reinforces the cooperative federalism model and may prompt a re-evaluation of current fiscal policies.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions
prelimsPoints: Article 280 of the Indian Constitution deals with the Finance Commission.
The Finance Commission recommends the distribution of net proceeds of taxes between the Union and the States.
Fiscal federalism refers to the division of financial powers and responsibilities between different levels of government.
Cooperative federalism emphasizes cooperation between the Centre and States.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional provisions governing Centre-State financial relations in India and the role of the Finance Commission.
Discuss the challenges to fiscal federalism in India, particularly concerning resource allocation and states' autonomy, in light of recent Supreme Court judgments.
Suggest reforms to strengthen cooperative fiscal federalism, ensuring equitable resource distribution and promoting balanced regional development.
articles.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building facade.
articles.trivia: The Finance Commission is constituted by the President of India every fifth year or at such earlier time as he considers necessary.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite EOS-08
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched its advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-08) aboard the Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV-C58) from Sriharikota.
• EOS-08 is designed to provide high-resolution imagery for applications in agriculture, forestry, water resources, and disaster management, enhancing India's remote sensing capabilities.
• The satellite features improved spectral bands and spatial resolution compared to its predecessors, promising more precise data for environmental monitoring and urban planning.
• This mission underscores ISRO's continued commitment to developing indigenous space technology for national benefit and global scientific collaboration.
• The successful deployment further solidifies India's position as a leader in space technology and earth observation.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space

Launched by ISRO using PSLV-C58.
Applications include agriculture, forestry, water resources, and disaster management.
Sriharikota is the primary spaceport of ISRO.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's Earth Observation Satellite program for national development and strategic autonomy.
Analyze how advancements in remote sensing technology, like EOS-08, can contribute to sustainable resource management and disaster mitigation in India.
Evaluate the role of ISRO in promoting scientific research, technological innovation, and international cooperation in the space sector.
articles.imageDescription: A rocket launching into space from a launchpad, with 'ISRO' visible.
articles.trivia: ISRO has launched over 100 Earth Observation Satellites since the inception of its remote sensing program in the 1970s.

articles.headline: Government Unveils National Urban Mobility Policy 2026 Focusing on Green Transport
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched the National Urban Mobility Policy (NUMP) 2026, aiming to transform urban transportation in India.
• The policy emphasizes a shift towards sustainable and green mobility solutions, including electric vehicles, public transport, cycling infrastructure, and pedestrian-friendly zones.
• Key objectives include reducing traffic congestion, improving air quality, enhancing accessibility for all citizens, and promoting transit-oriented development (TOD).
• It proposes significant investments in multimodal transport systems and encourages private sector participation in developing smart mobility solutions.
• The NUMP 2026 is aligned with India's climate commitments and the vision of smart cities, seeking to create efficient, equitable, and environmentally sound urban mobility ecosystems.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
prelimsPoints: NUMP 2026 launched by Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs.
Focuses on sustainable and green mobility.

mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the provisions of the National Urban Mobility Policy 2026 and its potential impact on urban planning and infrastructure development in India.
Discuss the challenges in implementing green transport solutions in Indian cities, considering factors like public acceptance, infrastructure costs, and technological integration.
Suggest policy interventions and community participation strategies to foster a sustainable urban mobility culture in rapidly growing Indian cities.
articles.imageDescription: A modern city street with electric buses, bicycles, and pedestrians.
articles.trivia: Globally, urban transport accounts for a significant portion of greenhouse gas emissions, making sustainable mobility crucial for climate action.

articles.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Contribution to Global Climate Adaptation Fund
articles.explainer:
• India announced a significant increase in its financial contribution to the Global Climate Adaptation Fund, reaffirming its commitment to international climate action.
• The pledge comes amidst growing concerns over the escalating impacts of climate change, particularly in developing nations and vulnerable regions.
• India emphasized the critical need for equitable burden-sharing in climate finance, urging developed countries to meet their historical responsibilities.
• The Adaptation Fund supports concrete adaptation projects and programmes in developing countries that are particularly vulnerable to the adverse effects of climate change.
• This move signals India's leadership in advocating for climate justice and collective efforts to build resilience against environmental challenges.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: The Adaptation Fund was established under the Kyoto Protocol.
It primarily serves the Paris Agreement.
Funds concrete adaptation projects in vulnerable developing countries.
India is a signatory to the Paris Agreement.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's role in global climate negotiations, particularly its stance on climate finance and adaptation.
Discuss the challenges faced by developing countries in adapting to climate change and the importance of international financial support.
Evaluate the effectiveness of global climate funds, such as the Adaptation Fund, in achieving their objectives and promoting climate resilience.
articles.imageDescription: A globe showing extreme weather events and a hand extending financial aid.
articles.trivia: The Adaptation Fund is unique in its direct access modality, allowing eligible developing countries to access financing directly through their national implementing entities.

articles.headline: RBI Governor Signals Vigilance on Inflation, Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Global Uncertainties
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Governor indicated a cautious stance on monetary policy, opting to maintain the benchmark repo rate at its current level.
• The decision comes as the RBI monitors evolving inflationary pressures, both domestic and global, alongside geopolitical uncertainties impacting commodity prices.
• The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth, emphasizing flexibility in future actions.
• Experts suggest the RBI is balancing the need to control inflation with ensuring adequate liquidity to support economic recovery.
• The central bank also highlighted concerns over global economic slowdowns and their potential spillover effects on India's financial stability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) determines the policy interest rates.

mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and instruments of monetary policy in India, with a focus on the role of the RBI and MPC.
Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth amidst global uncertainties and supply-side shocks.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the current inflation targeting framework in achieving price stability and fostering sustainable economic development in India.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building with its logo.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members: three from the RBI and three external members appointed by the government.

articles.headline: 'Operation Suraksha' Targets Cybercrime Syndicates Across Multiple States
articles.explainer:
• A large-scale coordinated law enforcement drive, dubbed 'Operation Suraksha', was launched simultaneously across several states to dismantle major cybercrime syndicates.
• The operation involved federal agencies and state police forces, leading to numerous arrests and the seizure of digital evidence, targeting online financial fraud, phishing, and data theft.
• This multi-agency effort highlights the growing sophistication of cyber criminal networks and the imperative for enhanced inter-state and national cooperation.
• Authorities emphasized the need for public awareness campaigns to educate citizens about cyber threats and safe online practices.
• The success of 'Operation Suraksha' is seen as a significant step in bolstering India's cyber security infrastructure and protecting citizens from digital exploitation.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism

Suggest comprehensive strategies, including technological advancements, international cooperation, and public awareness, to strengthen India's cyber security posture.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image of a hacker and digital security icons.
articles.trivia: India recorded over 50,000 cybercrime incidents in the first half of 2025, with financial fraud being the most prevalent type.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Greater Transparency in Electoral Bond Scheme
articles.explainer:
• A parliamentary standing committee submitted its report recommending significant reforms to the Electoral Bond Scheme to enhance transparency and accountability in political funding.
• The report highlighted concerns raised by various stakeholders regarding the anonymity of donors and its potential impact on fair elections.
• Key recommendations include mandating disclosure of donor identities to the Election Commission of India (ECI) and reducing the anonymity period for bonds.
• The committee emphasized the need for a robust mechanism to track political donations to prevent illicit funding and ensure a level playing field for all political parties.
• This move aims to address long-standing criticisms of the scheme and strengthen democratic processes by promoting cleaner political financing.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation

Bonds are issued by State Bank of India (SBI).
Allows anonymous donations to political parties.
Election Commission of India (ECI) is responsible for conducting elections.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the Electoral Bond Scheme, discussing its stated objectives versus its impact on transparency and democratic fairness.
Examine the role of the Election Commission of India and the judiciary in addressing concerns related to political funding and electoral reforms.
Propose alternative mechanisms for political funding that can ensure both transparency and discourage illicit money in elections, drawing comparisons with international practices.
articles.imageDescription: A ballot box with currency notes flowing into it, symbolizing political funding.
articles.trivia: The minimum denomination for electoral bonds is ₹1,000, and the maximum is ₹1 crore.

articles.headline: Report Highlights Intensifying Water Scarcity in Peninsular India
articles.explainer:
• A recent report by the National Water Commission has raised alarm over the escalating water scarcity crisis in several regions of Peninsular India.
• The report attributes the crisis to erratic monsoon patterns, over-extraction of groundwater, rapid urbanization, and inadequate water management practices.
• It projects a significant increase in water stress for states like Maharashtra, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, and Andhra Pradesh in the coming decades.
• Recommendations include promoting water conservation, adopting efficient irrigation techniques, rejuvenating traditional water bodies, and implementing robust rainwater harvesting systems.
• The findings underscore the urgent need for integrated water resource management and inter-state cooperation to ensure water security for the affected regions.
gsSubTopics: Distribution of key natural resources across the world (including South Asia and the Indian subcontinent); factors responsible for the location of primary, secondary, and tertiary sector industries in various parts of the world (including India)

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.

mainsPoints: Analyze the multifaceted causes of water scarcity in Peninsular India, linking them to geographical, climatic, and anthropogenic factors.

Suggest comprehensive and sustainable strategies for water resource management, including policy reforms, technological solutions, and community participation, to address the crisis.
articles.imageDescription: A parched, cracked earth with a small, almost dry water body.
articles.trivia: India is home to 18% of the world's population but has only 4% of the world's freshwater resources.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey Uncovers Ancient Buddhist Site in Odisha
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a significant ancient Buddhist site in the Jajpur district of Odisha.
• Excavations have revealed remnants of stupas, viharas, and intricate sculptures, suggesting a thriving Buddhist monastic complex dating back to the 7th-9th centuries CE.
• The findings include rare inscriptions and artifacts that provide new insights into the spread of Buddhism in Eastern India and its artistic expressions.
• This discovery adds to Odisha's rich Buddhist heritage, which already includes prominent sites like Lalitgiri, Udayagiri, and Ratnagiri, often referred to as the 'Diamond Triangle' of Buddhism.
• The site is expected to become a major focus for further research and conservation efforts, potentially boosting cultural tourism in the region.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.

mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing the history and cultural landscape of ancient India, particularly concerning the spread of Buddhism.
Analyze the unique architectural and artistic features of Buddhist sites in Eastern India, comparing them with other regions.
Evaluate the challenges and strategies involved in conserving and promoting India's rich archaeological heritage for cultural tourism and academic research.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating ancient ruins at a historical site.
articles.trivia: Jajpur district in Odisha is often considered a hub of ancient Buddhist activities, with several monasteries and stupas discovered over the years.

articles.headline: India Hosts Global Conference on Digital Public Infrastructure for Inclusive Growth
articles.explainer:
• India hosted a major international conference focusing on the role of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) in fostering inclusive growth and sustainable development globally.
• The event brought together policymakers, technology experts, and international organizations to share best practices and forge collaborations on building robust digital ecosystems.
• India showcased its successful DPI models, such as Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, as examples of how technology can empower citizens and streamline public services.
• Discussions centered on the ethical implications of DPI, data governance frameworks, and ensuring equitable access to digital services in developing nations.
• The conference underscored India's growing influence in the global digital governance landscape and its commitment to leveraging technology for societal good.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation

Evaluate India's role in shaping global digital governance norms and promoting responsible use of technology for sustainable development.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized representation of digital networks connecting people and services.
articles.trivia: India's UPI system processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month in 2025, making it one of the world's fastest-growing digital payment platforms.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'PM-Kisan Samriddhi' Scheme to Boost Small Farmer Incomes
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government launched a new welfare initiative, 'PM-Kisan Samriddhi Yojana', aimed at enhancing the income and financial stability of small and marginal farmers.
• The scheme introduces direct benefit transfers (DBT) for agricultural input subsidies, crop insurance premiums, and access to modern farming equipment at subsidized rates.
• It integrates elements of existing schemes like PM-KISAN and aims to streamline financial support to farmers, reducing bureaucratic hurdles.
• Focus areas include promoting sustainable farming practices, encouraging crop diversification, and providing market linkages for agricultural produce.
• 'PM-Kisan Samriddhi' is designed to address the persistent challenges faced by the agricultural sector, fostering rural prosperity and food security.

prelimsPoints: PM-Kisan Samriddhi Yojana targets small and marginal farmers.

mainsPoints: Critically analyze the effectiveness of direct income support schemes like PM-KISAN and its enhanced version, PM-Kisan Samriddhi, in addressing agrarian distress in India.
Discuss the challenges in implementing large-scale agricultural welfare schemes, including issues of targeting, financial inclusion, and bureaucratic efficiency.

articles.imageDescription: A farmer working in a field with a tractor, symbolizing agricultural development.
articles.trivia: PM-KISAN (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi) was launched in 2019, providing income support of ₹6,000 per year to eligible farmer families.

articles.headline: Border Roads Organisation Completes Strategic Tunnel in Arunachal Pradesh
articles.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) announced the successful completion of a strategically vital tunnel project in the challenging terrain of Arunachal Pradesh.
• The all-weather tunnel significantly enhances connectivity to forward areas along the Line of Actual Control (LAC), reducing travel time and improving logistical support for troops.
• Engineered to withstand extreme weather conditions and seismic activity, the tunnel is a testament to India's commitment to strengthening border infrastructure.
• This project is part of a broader initiative to upgrade critical infrastructure in border regions, crucial for national security and regional development.
• The new tunnel is expected to facilitate economic activities and tourism in remote areas, integrating them more closely with the national mainstream.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.

Evaluate the impact of improved border connectivity on regional development, tourism, and the socio-economic upliftment of communities residing in these areas.
articles.imageDescription: A newly constructed tunnel entrance in a mountainous, snowy region.
articles.trivia: The BRO was formed in 1960 to secure India's borders and develop infrastructure in remote areas of the north and north-east states.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Achieves Milestone of 500 Million Health IDs
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) announced a significant milestone, having successfully generated over 500 million Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA) IDs.
• This achievement marks a major step towards creating a comprehensive digital health ecosystem in India, enabling seamless access to health records and services.
• The ABHA ID allows individuals to link their health records across various healthcare providers, facilitating better diagnosis, treatment, and continuity of care.
• The mission aims to standardize digital health data, promote interoperability, and empower citizens with control over their health information.
• This milestone reflects the rapid adoption of digital health solutions and the government's commitment to universal health coverage through technology.

articles.headline: Government Introduces New Guidelines for Gig Workers' Welfare and Social Security
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Labour and Employment unveiled a comprehensive set of guidelines aimed at ensuring better welfare and social security for India's burgeoning gig workforce.
• The guidelines propose a framework for platform-based workers, mandating certain benefits like accident insurance, health coverage, and skill development programs from aggregators.
• It also addresses grievances redressal mechanisms and aims to provide greater clarity on the employment status of gig workers, a long-standing demand.
• This move follows extensive consultations with gig worker unions, industry representatives, and legal experts, acknowledging the unique challenges of the gig economy.
• The new guidelines are a significant step towards formalizing the gig economy and providing a safety net for millions of workers who often lack traditional employment benefits.

articles.headline: India-Sri Lanka Joint Maritime Security Exercise 'Sagar Mitra' Concludes Successfully
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-Sri Lanka joint maritime security exercise, 'Sagar Mitra', concluded successfully in the Bay of Bengal, enhancing bilateral naval cooperation.
• The exercise focused on improving interoperability, information sharing, and coordination in areas like maritime surveillance, anti-piracy operations, and disaster relief.
• Both navies conducted complex tactical maneuvers, search and rescue drills, and communication exercises, reinforcing their commitment to regional security.
• 'Sagar Mitra' underscores the growing strategic partnership between India and Sri Lanka, particularly in safeguarding vital sea lanes and addressing common maritime challenges.
• The exercise is crucial for maintaining peace and stability in the Indian Ocean Region, strengthening collective response capabilities against non-traditional threats.

articles.headline: Government Drafts New Policy to Boost Exploration and Mining of Critical Minerals
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Mines has drafted a new policy aimed at accelerating the exploration and mining of critical minerals essential for India's energy transition and strategic industries.
• The policy seeks to attract private investment, streamline regulatory clearances, and incentivize advanced exploration technologies to unlock India's mineral potential.
• Critical minerals like lithium, cobalt, nickel, and rare earth elements are vital for manufacturing electric vehicles, renewable energy components, and defense equipment.
• The move is part of India's broader strategy to reduce import dependence and establish secure supply chains for these crucial resources.
• The policy emphasizes sustainable mining practices and value addition within India, aiming to create a robust domestic critical mineral ecosystem.

articles.headline: Gender Budgeting Report Highlights Gaps in Allocation and Implementation
articles.explainer:
• The annual Gender Budgeting Report, released by the Ministry of Finance, indicated persistent gaps in the allocation and effective implementation of funds for women-centric schemes.
• While the overall allocation for gender-responsive budgeting has shown an increase, the report pointed out challenges in tracking the actual utilization and outcomes at the ground level.
• It emphasized the need for better capacity building among implementing agencies and more robust monitoring mechanisms to ensure funds reach the intended beneficiaries.
• The report also recommended a more holistic approach to gender budgeting, moving beyond mere expenditure to focus on empowerment and equity across all sectors.
• This critical analysis aims to improve the effectiveness of government initiatives in promoting gender equality and women's empowerment.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Inclusive Global AI Governance Framework at UN Summit
articles.explainer:
• India's representative at a high-level UN summit on Artificial Intelligence (AI) emphasized the need for an inclusive and equitable global governance framework for AI.
• India advocated for a human-centric approach to AI development, ensuring that its benefits are shared widely and that ethical considerations are paramount.
• The discussions focused on addressing potential biases in AI, ensuring data privacy, and preventing the misuse of AI technologies for harmful purposes.
• India highlighted its 'AI for All' strategy, promoting responsible AI innovation that aligns with national development goals and serves global good.
• This stance positions India as a key player in shaping international norms and standards for AI, advocating for a multi-stakeholder approach to its regulation.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Electoral Bond Scheme' in India, consider the following statements:
1. The scheme was introduced in 2017 to allow anonymous donations to political parties.
2. Electoral bonds are issued by all scheduled commercial banks in India.
3. The scheme aims to enhance transparency in political funding by making donor identities public to the Election Commission of India.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Electoral Bond Scheme was introduced in 2017 to allow anonymous donations to political parties. Statement 2 is incorrect: Electoral bonds are issued only by the State Bank of India (SBI). Statement 3 is incorrect: The scheme allows for anonymity of donors, which has been a point of criticism, and parliamentary panels have recommended making donor identities public to the ECI.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for determining the benchmark repo rate in India?
options: (b) Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
(c) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the RBI
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the RBI
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is responsible for determining the policy interest rates, including the benchmark repo rate, to achieve the inflation target.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM):
1. It aims to create a nationwide digital health ecosystem.
2. The Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA) ID allows individuals to link their health records across various healthcare providers.
3. The National Health Authority (NHA) is the implementing agency for ABDM.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The ABDM aims to create a digital health ecosystem in India. The ABHA ID is a core component enabling health record linking. The National Health Authority (NHA) is indeed the implementing agency.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Adaptation Fund', consider the following statements:
1. It was established under the Kyoto Protocol and primarily serves the Paris Agreement.
2. It provides financial support for concrete adaptation projects and programmes in developing countries.
3. Developed countries are the sole contributors to the fund.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Adaptation Fund was established under the Kyoto Protocol and now primarily serves the Paris Agreement. Statement 2 is correct: Its mandate is to finance concrete adaptation projects and programmes in developing countries that are particularly vulnerable to the adverse effects of climate change. Statement 3 is incorrect: While developed countries are major contributors, other sources of funding include voluntary contributions from developed country Parties, and a share of the proceeds from the Clean Development Mechanism (CDM) project activities.

GS1.headline: Report Highlights Intensifying Water Scarcity in Peninsular India
GS1.explainer:
• A recent report by the National Water Commission has raised alarm over the escalating water scarcity crisis in several regions of Peninsular India.
• The report attributes the crisis to erratic monsoon patterns, over-extraction of groundwater, rapid urbanization, and inadequate water management practices.
• It projects a significant increase in water stress for states like Maharashtra, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, and Andhra Pradesh in the coming decades.
• Recommendations include promoting water conservation, adopting efficient irrigation techniques, rejuvenating traditional water bodies, and implementing robust rainwater harvesting systems.
• The findings underscore the urgent need for integrated water resource management and inter-state cooperation to ensure water security for the affected regions.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey Uncovers Ancient Buddhist Site in Odisha
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a significant ancient Buddhist site in the Jajpur district of Odisha.
• Excavations have revealed remnants of stupas, viharas, and intricate sculptures, suggesting a thriving Buddhist monastic complex dating back to the 7th-9th centuries CE.
• The findings include rare inscriptions and artifacts that provide new insights into the spread of Buddhism in Eastern India and its artistic expressions.
• This discovery adds to Odisha's rich Buddhist heritage, which already includes prominent sites like Lalitgiri, Udayagiri, and Ratnagiri, often referred to as the 'Diamond Triangle' of Buddhism.
• The site is expected to become a major focus for further research and conservation efforts, potentially boosting cultural tourism in the region.

GS2.explainer:

• Sources indicate progress on intellectual property rights, data protection, and market access for certain agricultural and automotive products.

• A joint statement from lead negotiators highlighted a shared commitment to a balanced and mutually beneficial outcome.
• The finalization of the FTA could mark a strategic shift in India's trade policy, diversifying its economic partnerships.

GS2.headline: Veteran Politician Mukul Roy Passes Away at 71
GS2.explainer:
• Mukul Roy, a prominent political figure known for his significant contributions to West Bengal politics and Indian Railways, passed away at the age of 71.
• Roy served as a Member of Parliament (Rajya Sabha) from 2006 to 2017 and held the crucial portfolio of Minister of Railways in 2012.
• He was also a sitting Member of the Legislative Assembly (MLA) in West Bengal since 2021, representing the Krishnanagar Uttar constituency.
• Beginning his career with the Indian National Congress, Roy later became a founding member of the Trinamool Congress (TMC) and held several key positions within the party.
• His political journey also saw a brief stint with the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) before returning to the TMC, highlighting his dynamic political career.

GS2.trivia: Mukul Roy was instrumental in the growth of the Trinamool Congress (TMC) and was often referred to as Mamata Banerjee's second-in-command during the party's early years.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds States' Rights in Landmark Fiscal Devolution Case
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a significant judgment, affirming the constitutional principle of fiscal federalism by ruling in favor of several states regarding the Centre's allocation of financial resources.
• The case centered on the interpretation of Article 280 (Finance Commission) and the extent of the Union government's discretion in distributing tax revenues and grants-in-aid.
• The Court emphasized the need for a balanced approach to federal financial relations, ensuring states have adequate resources for developmental activities without undue central interference.
• This ruling is expected to have far-reaching implications for Centre-State financial dynamics, potentially leading to a more equitable distribution of funds.
• Legal experts suggest the judgment reinforces the cooperative federalism model and may prompt a re-evaluation of current fiscal policies.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein

mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional provisions governing Centre-State financial relations in India and the role of the Finance Commission.
Discuss the challenges to fiscal federalism in India, particularly concerning resource allocation and states' autonomy, in light of recent Supreme Court judgments.

GS2.trivia: The Finance Commission is constituted by the President of India every fifth year or at such earlier time as he considers necessary.

GS2.headline: Government Unveils National Urban Mobility Policy 2026 Focusing on Green Transport
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched the National Urban Mobility Policy (NUMP) 2026, aiming to transform urban transportation in India.
• The policy emphasizes a shift towards sustainable and green mobility solutions, including electric vehicles, public transport, cycling infrastructure, and pedestrian-friendly zones.
• Key objectives include reducing traffic congestion, improving air quality, enhancing accessibility for all citizens, and promoting transit-oriented development (TOD).
• It proposes significant investments in multimodal transport systems and encourages private sector participation in developing smart mobility solutions.
• The NUMP 2026 is aligned with India's climate commitments and the vision of smart cities, seeking to create efficient, equitable, and environmentally sound urban mobility ecosystems.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Greater Transparency in Electoral Bond Scheme
GS2.explainer:
• A parliamentary standing committee submitted its report recommending significant reforms to the Electoral Bond Scheme to enhance transparency and accountability in political funding.
• The report highlighted concerns raised by various stakeholders regarding the anonymity of donors and its potential impact on fair elections.
• Key recommendations include mandating disclosure of donor identities to the Election Commission of India (ECI) and reducing the anonymity period for bonds.
• The committee emphasized the need for a robust mechanism to track political donations to prevent illicit funding and ensure a level playing field for all political parties.
• This move aims to address long-standing criticisms of the scheme and strengthen democratic processes by promoting cleaner political financing.

GS2.headline: India Hosts Global Conference on Digital Public Infrastructure for Inclusive Growth
GS2.explainer:
• India hosted a major international conference focusing on the role of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) in fostering inclusive growth and sustainable development globally.
• The event brought together policymakers, technology experts, and international organizations to share best practices and forge collaborations on building robust digital ecosystems.
• India showcased its successful DPI models, such as Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, as examples of how technology can empower citizens and streamline public services.
• Discussions centered on the ethical implications of DPI, data governance frameworks, and ensuring equitable access to digital services in developing nations.
• The conference underscored India's growing influence in the global digital governance landscape and its commitment to leveraging technology for societal good.

• This milestone reflects the rapid adoption of digital health solutions and the government's commitment to universal health coverage through technology.

GS2.headline: India-Sri Lanka Joint Maritime Security Exercise 'Sagar Mitra' Concludes Successfully
GS2.explainer:

• The exercise focused on improving interoperability, information sharing, and coordination in areas like maritime surveillance, anti-piracy operations, and disaster relief.
• Both navies conducted complex tactical maneuvers, search and rescue drills, and communication exercises, reinforcing their commitment to regional security.
• 'Sagar Mitra' underscores the growing strategic partnership between India and Sri Lanka, particularly in safeguarding vital sea lanes and addressing common maritime challenges.
• The exercise is crucial for maintaining peace and stability in the Indian Ocean Region, strengthening collective response capabilities against non-traditional threats.

GS2.headline: India Advocates for Inclusive Global AI Governance Framework at UN Summit
GS2.explainer:
• India's representative at a high-level UN summit on Artificial Intelligence (AI) emphasized the need for an inclusive and equitable global governance framework for AI.
• India advocated for a human-centric approach to AI development, ensuring that its benefits are shared widely and that ethical considerations are paramount.
• The discussions focused on addressing potential biases in AI, ensuring data privacy, and preventing the misuse of AI technologies for harmful purposes.
• India highlighted its 'AI for All' strategy, promoting responsible AI innovation that aligns with national development goals and serves global good.
• This stance positions India as a key player in shaping international norms and standards for AI, advocating for a multi-stakeholder approach to its regulation.

GS3.headline: Air Ambulance Crash Near Ranchi Kills All Seven On Board; DGCA Orders Probe
GS3.explainer:
• A Beechcraft C90 air ambulance operated by Redbird Airways crashed into a forest shortly after takeoff from Ranchi's Birsa Munda Airport.
• The tragic incident occurred during a medical evacuation mission, resulting in the immediate death of all seven individuals on board, including patients, medical staff, and crew.
• Preliminary reports suggest engine trouble or a sudden loss of control as potential causes, though a detailed investigation is underway.
• The Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) has initiated a high-level inquiry to ascertain the exact reasons behind the crash and to review aviation safety protocols for air ambulances.
• This incident reignites concerns about the safety standards and regulatory oversight of non-scheduled air operations, particularly those involving critical medical transport.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite EOS-08
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched its advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-08) aboard the Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV-C58) from Sriharikota.
• EOS-08 is designed to provide high-resolution imagery for applications in agriculture, forestry, water resources, and disaster management, enhancing India's remote sensing capabilities.
• The satellite features improved spectral bands and spatial resolution compared to its predecessors, promising more precise data for environmental monitoring and urban planning.
• This mission underscores ISRO's continued commitment to developing indigenous space technology for national benefit and global scientific collaboration.
• The successful deployment further solidifies India's position as a leader in space technology and earth observation.

GS3.trivia: ISRO has launched over 100 Earth Observation Satellites since the inception of its remote sensing program in the 1970s.

GS3.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Contribution to Global Climate Adaptation Fund
GS3.explainer:
• India announced a significant increase in its financial contribution to the Global Climate Adaptation Fund, reaffirming its commitment to international climate action.
• The pledge comes amidst growing concerns over the escalating impacts of climate change, particularly in developing nations and vulnerable regions.
• India emphasized the critical need for equitable burden-sharing in climate finance, urging developed countries to meet their historical responsibilities.
• The Adaptation Fund supports concrete adaptation projects and programmes in developing countries that are particularly vulnerable to the adverse effects of climate change.
• This move signals India's leadership in advocating for climate justice and collective efforts to build resilience against environmental challenges.

GS3.headline: RBI Governor Signals Vigilance on Inflation, Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Global Uncertainties
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Governor indicated a cautious stance on monetary policy, opting to maintain the benchmark repo rate at its current level.
• The decision comes as the RBI monitors evolving inflationary pressures, both domestic and global, alongside geopolitical uncertainties impacting commodity prices.
• The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth, emphasizing flexibility in future actions.
• Experts suggest the RBI is balancing the need to control inflation with ensuring adequate liquidity to support economic recovery.
• The central bank also highlighted concerns over global economic slowdowns and their potential spillover effects on India's financial stability.

GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members: three from the RBI and three external members appointed by the government.

GS3.headline: 'Operation Suraksha' Targets Cybercrime Syndicates Across Multiple States
GS3.explainer:
• A large-scale coordinated law enforcement drive, dubbed 'Operation Suraksha', was launched simultaneously across several states to dismantle major cybercrime syndicates.
• The operation involved federal agencies and state police forces, leading to numerous arrests and the seizure of digital evidence, targeting online financial fraud, phishing, and data theft.
• This multi-agency effort highlights the growing sophistication of cyber criminal networks and the imperative for enhanced inter-state and national cooperation.
• Authorities emphasized the need for public awareness campaigns to educate citizens about cyber threats and safe online practices.
• The success of 'Operation Suraksha' is seen as a significant step in bolstering India's cyber security infrastructure and protecting citizens from digital exploitation.

GS3.headline: Government Launches 'PM-Kisan Samriddhi' Scheme to Boost Small Farmer Incomes
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government launched a new welfare initiative, 'PM-Kisan Samriddhi Yojana', aimed at enhancing the income and financial stability of small and marginal farmers.
• The scheme introduces direct benefit transfers (DBT) for agricultural input subsidies, crop insurance premiums, and access to modern farming equipment at subsidized rates.
• It integrates elements of existing schemes like PM-KISAN and aims to streamline financial support to farmers, reducing bureaucratic hurdles.
• Focus areas include promoting sustainable farming practices, encouraging crop diversification, and providing market linkages for agricultural produce.
• 'PM-Kisan Samriddhi' is designed to address the persistent challenges faced by the agricultural sector, fostering rural prosperity and food security.

GS3.headline: Border Roads Organisation Completes Strategic Tunnel in Arunachal Pradesh
GS3.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) announced the successful completion of a strategically vital tunnel project in the challenging terrain of Arunachal Pradesh.
• The all-weather tunnel significantly enhances connectivity to forward areas along the Line of Actual Control (LAC), reducing travel time and improving logistical support for troops.
• Engineered to withstand extreme weather conditions and seismic activity, the tunnel is a testament to India's commitment to strengthening border infrastructure.
• This project is part of a broader initiative to upgrade critical infrastructure in border regions, crucial for national security and regional development.
• The new tunnel is expected to facilitate economic activities and tourism in remote areas, integrating them more closely with the national mainstream.

GS3.headline: Government Introduces New Guidelines for Gig Workers' Welfare and Social Security
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Labour and Employment unveiled a comprehensive set of guidelines aimed at ensuring better welfare and social security for India's burgeoning gig workforce.
• The guidelines propose a framework for platform-based workers, mandating certain benefits like accident insurance, health coverage, and skill development programs from aggregators.
• It also addresses grievances redressal mechanisms and aims to provide greater clarity on the employment status of gig workers, a long-standing demand.
• This move follows extensive consultations with gig worker unions, industry representatives, and legal experts, acknowledging the unique challenges of the gig economy.
• The new guidelines are a significant step towards formalizing the gig economy and providing a safety net for millions of workers who often lack traditional employment benefits.

GS3.headline: Government Drafts New Policy to Boost Exploration and Mining of Critical Minerals
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Mines has drafted a new policy aimed at accelerating the exploration and mining of critical minerals essential for India's energy transition and strategic industries.
• The policy seeks to attract private investment, streamline regulatory clearances, and incentivize advanced exploration technologies to unlock India's mineral potential.
• Critical minerals like lithium, cobalt, nickel, and rare earth elements are vital for manufacturing electric vehicles, renewable energy components, and defense equipment.
• The move is part of India's broader strategy to reduce import dependence and establish secure supply chains for these crucial resources.
• The policy emphasizes sustainable mining practices and value addition within India, aiming to create a robust domestic critical mineral ecosystem.
